programme.desc: 5/6. A break in the weather at the end of January is good for everyone. Plus a famous name who's made his home in Harris as an engineer on the VW Beetle.

programme.title: Duncan Mackenzie | Dìleab nam Beann
programme.desc: The story of Duncan Mackenzie's passion for the landscape of north west Scotland. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Trusadh - Air ais gu Nadar (Back to Nature)
programme.desc: 2/14. An tarraing làidir a th' aig nàdar do mhac an duine. A documentary examining the appeal of the great outdoors.

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: 10/13. Drama Gàidhlig stèidhichte ann an Leòdhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: 5/10. A tourist and his wife visit Fraochy Bay and decide to pay a visit to the local museum with its ambitious exhibits relating to local history.

programme.title: Alleluia!/Spiritual Praise
programme.desc: 6/6. Iain MacKinnon presents a spiritual mix of hymns, psalms and readings. Isobel Anne Martin, Donald-Iain Brown and Ruairidh Gray are among the singers.

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: Sealladh ùr bho Alba. A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: Sealladh ùr bho Alba. A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Oscar & Ealasaid
programme.desc: 4/8. Celestine registers Ernest to a music contest without telling him.

programme.title: Meaban is Moo
programme.desc: 9/10. Tha Meaban is Moo a' gabhail chun an àrd-ùrlair. Meaban and Moo are busy preparing a concert for Linda.

programme.title: Orain CBeebies
programme.desc: 5/7. Taghadh farsaing de dh'òrain bho Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh agus sgioba CBeebies ALBA. A selection of songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters.

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: 8/15. Eilidh Cormack meets young pet owners who share how they care for their pets at home. In this episode, Eilidh is joined by Findlay and Lachlan, and their pet dogs Maisie and Lola.

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais (Big Barn Farm)
programme.desc: 18/20. Tha aoidh spaideil air chèilidh air an tuathanas ach saoil an còrd a' chùis rithe? There's a posh visitor at the farm today, but will she enjoy the experience?

programme.title: Gasta a' Ghraineag/Happy the Hoglet
programme.desc: 25/26. Tha eagal air Màgan is Fionnlagh bho rudan eagalach an-diugh. Màgan and Fionnlagh are scared of frightening things today.

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: 49/52. Tha Giomach a' bruidhinn rinn mu dhèidhinn fhèin an-diugh. A Lobster tells us all about himself today.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: 8/80. Catriona MacPhee reads Edie, written and illustrated by Sophy Henn and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.

programme.title: Clann na Cruinne
programme.desc: 9/12. Tha Stella airson a bhith am measg nam beann - aig tèarmann. Stella loves being in the mountains - at a refuge.

programme.title: Flix
programme.desc: 5/13. There's been calamity. Flix has lost his sense of smell. What will he do now? Tha mòr-chall air a bhith ann. Tha Flix air àile a chall. Dè nì e a-nis?

programme.title: Dùbhlain CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: 4/6. Calum Macdonald takes a look at some of the bonkers challenges attempted by the CBBC ALBA team, including some very smelly cheese tasting!

programme.title: Seonaidh/Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: 5/20. Bitzer is left with a mountain of work when the farmer is rendered incapable. Enter the farmhand who is more interested in his mobile phone than getting the task done.

programme.title: Bogaisean is...
programme.desc: ...Gumbalan/Bottersnikes and Gumbles. 35/52. Tha Sona cho toilichte an-diugh. Sona is very happy today.

programme.title: Fuine
programme.desc: 4/6. Featuring a baked Alaska with its mountain of meringue, as well as a puff pastry parcel stuffed with cheese and vegetables.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: 17/26. Calum Maclean is in Fort William visiting an unexpected industrial site.

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich
programme.desc: 5/8. Heather Dewar meets Roddy John Macleod and draws his colour portrait. Fraoch Nic an Deòir cuide ri Ruairidh Iain MacLeòid airson a dhearbhadh gu dathte.

programme.title: Glan Fhèin
programme.desc: 2/3. A tempting offer from Lewis Fraser is on the table. The girls need to discuss selling the business, but right now they aren't speaking to each other. Contains adult humour.

programme.title: Conaltradh Creutairean/Wild Talk
programme.desc: 4/5. Sùil air mar a tha beathaichean a' conaltradh an uair a dh' fheumas iad obrachadh còmhla mar sgioba. A look at how animals communicate when they need to work together as a team.

programme.title: Skipinnish aig a' Chaisteal (Shorts)
programme.desc: 4/10. Skipinnish le December aig cuirm-chiùil bho Chaisteal Dhùn Èideann. Skipinnish perform December at a concert from Edinburgh Castle.

programme.title: Srùb: Scotland on Coffee
programme.desc: Thig air turas sònraichte cuide ri Ellen NicDhòmhnaill 's i ag ionnsachadh a h-uile sìon mu chofaidh. Join Ellen MacDonald on a journey to discover all there is to know about coffee.

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions: Dluth an Daimh
programme.desc: 5/6. Muireann performs alongside Scottish singer-songwriter Iain Morrison, as well as songs from other musical stars including Iris DeMent and Cara Dillon.

programme.title: Ibsen: The Lady from the Sea (1974)
programme.desc: The 1974 adaption of Henrik Ibsen's story of Ellida Wangel, her marriage issues and her pursuit of happiness and love elsewhere.

programme.title: Mañana
programme.desc: Broadcast on 1 February 1956, composer Arthur Benjamin's Mañana was the first opera commissioned by the BBC.

programme.title: Soundwaves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: 1/2. Dr Helen Czerski investigates the science behind the sounds we're familiar with and the sounds that we normally can't hear, from Big Ben to the Stromboli volcano.

programme.title: Come Dancing 1978: Grand Final
programme.desc: Terry Wogan hosts as Midlands and West take on North West in the 1978 Come Dancing grand final from the Lyceum Ballroom, London. With commentary by Barri Haynes.

programme.title: Wogan: The Best Of
programme.desc: 14/15. Sir Terry Wogan presents more magical moments from his days in the hotseat of Britain's best-loved chat show, with guests including Judi Dench, Helen Mirren and Bob Hoskins.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: 3/6. Series about the creatures that live in China's most beautiful landscapes. This episode looks at the remote Tibetan Plateau, home to chiru antelopes, wild yaks and bears.

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: 3/3. Samira Ahmed goes back to when Persia faced her gravest threat, Genghis Khan, and reveals how the Safavids, who succeeded the Mongols, give Iran a powerful new identity.

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson hosts as Frank Muir, Joanna Lumley and Miles Kington face Patrick Campbell, Rula Lenska and Ian Wooldridge in the panel game of word definitions and deceptions.

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Joyce Grenfell, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Lina Lalandi.

programme.title: Brian Cox: Seven Days on Mars
programme.desc: With unique access to Nasa, Brian Cox follows Perseverance rover's search for life on Mars during a critical seven-day period as it undertakes an epic journey across the red planet.

programme.title: Voyage to Mars: The Longest Goodbye
programme.desc: Film following Nasa psychologist Dr Al Holland as he prepares Mars-bound astronauts for the psychological demands of a three-year absence from Earth.

programme.title: The Race to Mars: Tomorrow's World
programme.desc: First transmitted in 1987, what is the future of manned space flight one year after the US Challenger disaster?

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Joyce Grenfell, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Lina Lalandi.

programme.title: Meet the Ancestors
programme.desc: 2/6. Archaeologist Julian Richards joins an excavation team as they uncover a rare find from a fourth-century Roman cemetery in Winchester: a lead coffin.

programme.title: Coastal Path

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: 6/8. Classic political sitcom. Jim Hacker faces embarrassment when Sir Humphrey tells a bigwig friend in the arts that the government is planning to cut grants.

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: 7/8. Jim Hacker decides to abolish the Department of Education and Science to reduce bureaucracy and save money, giving cash directly to schools. Sir Humphrey, of course, is appalled.

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth I: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: 3/4. Using 60 years of BBC archive, Vanessa Collingridge examines the life of Elizabeth Tudor.

programme.title: Price of Gold - Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary that revisits the 1994 scandal that embroiled competing US Olympic figure skaters Nancy Kerrigan and Tonya Harding.

programme.title: The Great American Buffalo
programme.desc: 2/2. By the late 1880s, the buffalo teeter on the brink of extinction.

programme.desc: Juno's No Nap Day. Juno decides to give up napping but soon has a cranky meltdown, as she is so tired.

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: The Weasy Family

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Ghostbasket. Dad is pretending to be an estate agent selling the Grannies' house to Mum.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Stumpfest. Dad and his mates hit the backyard ready to rip up some tree stumps!

programme.title: Piripenguins

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran

programme.title: Puffin Rock

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Metal Bug. Stop-motion animation. Small Clanger finds a strange ball while out fishing in space. No-one knows what it is, so Major decides to put it on a shelf in his workshop.

programme.title: BeddyByes

programme.title: In the Night Garden

programme.title: Daredevil
programme.desc: (2003) Superhero fantasy. Ben Affleck is a blind lawyer who turns vigilante by night. Jennifer Garner plays his lover and Colin Farrell the villain who endangers them both. Strong violence.

programme.title: Ghost Rider
programme.desc: (2007) Nicolas Cage is Marvel's soul-hunting, hot-headed-biker antihero. When Mephistopheles's son starts causing trouble in hell, Ghost Rider is forced to sort it out. With Peter Fonda.

programme.title: Riders of Justice
programme.desc: (2020) Darkly comic thriller with Mads Mikkelsen. A soldier recruits three geeks to help him hunt down his wife's killer. In Danish/subs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: LumoTV: Deaf Jams: Rhythm and...
programme.desc: ...Lyrics: Rapper MC Geezer teams up with drum 'n' bass icon Harry Shotta as best mates Luke and Lisa chase their dream.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: The Devil at 4 O'Clock
programme.desc: (1961) Drama. Spencer Tracy stars as an unfairly unpopular priest living on a volcanic South Sea island which is about to erupt. With Kerwin Matthews, Frank Sinatra and Barbara Luna.

programme.title: Censor Interview Special
programme.desc: Star Niamh Algar and director Prano Bailey-Bond talk about making their new psychological horror film, Censor.

programme.title: The Violent Men
programme.desc: (1955) Cold War-influenced western starring Glenn Ford and Edward G Robinson. A smallholder stands alone against the expansionist plans and brutal tactics of a tyrannical rancher.

programme.title: Arabesque
programme.desc: (1966) Gregory Peck stars in Stanley Donen's spy thriller. An Oxford professor is unwittingly drawn into a deadly web of political intrigue. With Sophia Loren and Alan Badel.

programme.title: Censor Interview Special
programme.desc: Star Niamh Algar and director Prano Bailey-Bond talk about making their new psychological horror film, Censor.

programme.title: Le Mans '66
programme.desc: (2019) Oscar-winning sports drama starring Matt Damon and Christian Bale. In the 60s, Ford set out to build a car that can defeat Ferrari at the 24 Hours of Le Mans race.

programme.title: Knock at the Cabin
programme.desc: (2023) M Night Shyamalan's jarringly intimate end-of-days horror with Dave Bautista. An armed group insist that a family of three must make an impossible life-or-death choice.

programme.title: Chevalier
programme.desc: (2022) Exuberant historical costume drama. Kelvin Harrison Jr stars as prodigiously talented French-Caribbean swordsman and composer Joseph Bologne, who literally out-fiddled Mozart.

programme.title: Blue Bayou
programme.desc: (2021) Justin Chon's poignant drama about a man of Korean descent who is threatened with deportation from the US even though he was adopted into an American family when he was three.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Santa Fe
programme.desc: (1951) Western starring Randolph Scott. Four brothers leave the defeated South to seek jobs as the US recovers from the Civil War. As one becomes a railwayman, the others turn to crime.

programme.title: The Black Arrow
programme.desc: (1948) Historical adventure. A man (Louis Hayward) returns from the Wars of the Roses to learn from his uncle that a family friend has killed his father. But is everything as it seems?

programme.title: To Hell and Back
programme.desc: (1955) Fact-based wartime drama. Audie Murphy, America's most decorated soldier in WWII, plays himself in this dramatic recreation of the valiant heroics that earned him the honours.

programme.title: Censor Interview Special
programme.desc: Star Niamh Algar and director Prano Bailey-Bond talk about making their new psychological horror film, Censor.

programme.title: Canyon Passage

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: (1994) Oscar-winning turn-of-the-century drama with Brad Pitt and Anthony Hopkins. A Montana patriarch's son falls for his brother's fiancée, setting off a tragic chain of events.

programme.title: A Man Called Otto
programme.desc: (2022) Darkly-tinged but ultimately feel-good comedy starring Tom Hanks as a grumpy misanthrope who spends his time badgering people into following society's rules. But why is he like this?

programme.title: Our Kind of Traitor
programme.desc: (2014) Thriller about a British couple who get drawn into a Russian mafia conspiracy. With Ewan McGregor, Naomie Harris, Stellan Skarsgård and Damian Lewis. Based on John le Carré's novel.

programme.title: Daredevil
